rounds.description: Diamond body with a triangle head on the left corner pointing straight left. The legs trail behind to the right, resembling a person leaning forward and running fast to the left.

Looks like a person facing left sitting down. The most distinct feature is the bottom right corner, which is a perfect 90-degree angle formed by a perfectly flat horizontal bottom edge and a straight vertical right edge.

Looks like a thick paper airplane or arrowhead pointing straight left. It has a sharp point on the far left, a single high peak on top, and its back edge slants smoothly down to a pointed tail at the bottom right.

Person sitting facing left. The bottom right corner is a perfect 90-degree angle with a flat bottom and a straight vertical right edge. It has a diamond head. The leftmost point is a triangle pointing diagonally down and to the left (no part points straight horizontally left).

Figure with a diamond head, leaning right. It has NO flat straight edges on its bottom or right side. Distinct features: a sharp triangular 'tail' pointing horizontally left from its mid-back, its lowest point is a tip pointing diagonally down-left, and another sharp point sticks out to the right.

Figure with a diamond head. Its most distinct feature is its entire left side, which is a single, unbroken, perfectly straight diagonal line slanting smoothly down to a sharp point at the bottom left. It also has a sharp triangular point sticking straight out horizontally to the right.

Has a perfect 90-degree bottom-right corner with a flat bottom and straight vertical right edge. It does NOT have a diamond head. Its top-most shape is a sharp triangle pointing horizontally left, and it has a second sharp point below that also pointing left.

Diamond head. The entire body below the head forms one large triangle. Its left edge is a single, unbroken, straight diagonal line slanting down-left all the way to the lowest sharp point. The top edge of the body (extending to the right of the head) is perfectly flat and horizontal, ending in a sharp point on the far right.

Diamond head. The bottom-right corner is a perfect 90-degree angle, formed by a perfectly flat horizontal bottom edge and a straight vertical right edge. The leftmost point aims diagonally down and to the left.

Diamond head at the top right. The absolute lowest part is a sharp tip pointing diagonally down-left (not straight down). The left side is jagged, featuring a prominent sharp triangle pointing perfectly straight horizontally left in the middle. A sharp point also sticks out on the right side. No flat bottom edge.

Central diamond shape. Attached to its left is a single sharp triangle pointing perfectly straight horizontally left. A thick solid block extends diagonally down and to the right from the diamond. The absolute lowest point of the entire figure is the sharp tip at the far bottom-right of this diagonal block. No straight vertical legs.

NO diamond shape. Looks like a paper airplane pointing left. The leftmost point is a sharp tip aiming perfectly straight horizontally left. Its most distinct feature is its ENTIRE back right edge: a single, perfectly straight, unbroken diagonal line slanting all the way down and to the right, from the high top peak to the sharp bottom-right tail. It has no flat horizontal bottom edge.

Diamond head. The ENTIRE LEFT outline of the whole figure (from the very top tip of the diamond down to the lowest bottom-left point) is a single, unbroken, perfectly straight diagonal line. Just below the head, a perfectly flat, horizontal top edge extends straight out to the RIGHT, ending in a sharp point. (Be careful not to pick the mirror image: the flat edge MUST point right).

Diamond head at the top right. The left side features a prominent sharp triangle pointing perfectly horizontally left. A sharp point also sticks out to the right. The absolute lowest part is a sharp tip pointing diagonally down-left (not straight down). No flat bottom edge.

Diamond head. The bottom-right corner is a perfect 90-degree angle, formed by a flat horizontal bottom edge and a straight vertical right edge. The leftmost part points diagonally down-left. Make sure the straight vertical edge is on the right, not the left!

Highest point is the top tip of a diamond. Attached to its left is a single sharp triangle pointing perfectly straight horizontally left. Extending from the diamond's bottom-right is ONE solid block slanting diagonally DOWN-RIGHT, ending in a sharp tip that is the lowest point of the whole figure. The space directly below the left-pointing triangle is empty (no leg pointing down-left). NO straight vertical edges and NO flat horizontal bottom edges anywhere.

NO diamond shape. Looks like a paper airplane pointing left. The leftmost point is a sharp tip aiming perfectly straight horizontally left. Its ENTIRE back right edge is a single, straight, unbroken diagonal line slanting all the way down and to the right, from the high top peak to the sharp bottom-right tail. It has no flat horizontal bottom edge.

NO diamond. Perfect 90-degree bottom-RIGHT corner (flat horizontal bottom, straight vertical right edge). The left side has exactly TWO sharp points sticking out, and BOTH aim perfectly straight HORIZONTALLY to the left (one at the top, one in the middle). Make sure the straight vertical edge is on the RIGHT!

Highest point is the top tip of a diamond. Attached to its left is a single sharp triangle pointing perfectly straight horizontally left. Extending from the diamond's bottom-right is ONE solid block slanting diagonally DOWN-RIGHT, ending in a sharp tip that is the lowest point of the whole figure. The space directly below the left-pointing triangle is empty (no leg pointing down-left). NO straight vertical edges and NO flat horizontal bottom edges anywhere.

Diamond head. The entire left outline is one continuous, perfectly straight DIAGONAL line slanting down-left to the very bottom (it is NOT vertical). Just below the head, a perfectly flat HORIZONTAL TOP EDGE extends straight to the RIGHT. There are NO flat bottom edges and NO vertical edges anywhere.

Absolutely NO diamond shape anywhere. Looks like a solid paper airplane pointing straight horizontally left. The highest point is a single sharp peak. Its ENTIRE back-right outline is one continuous, perfectly straight DIAGONAL line slanting smoothly down and to the right, from the top peak to the lowest bottom-right tip. NO flat horizontal edges and NO vertical straight edges anywhere.

Diamond head. The bottom-RIGHT corner is a perfect 90-degree angle formed by a perfectly flat horizontal bottom edge and a straight vertical right edge. The leftmost point of the entire figure aims diagonally down-left (it does NOT point straight horizontally left).

Diamond head at the top right. The left side features a prominent sharp triangle pointing perfectly horizontally left. A sharp point also sticks out to the right side. The absolute lowest part is a sharp tip pointing diagonally down-left (not straight down). NO flat bottom edges and NO straight vertical edges.

NO diamond. Perfect 90-degree bottom-RIGHT corner (flat horizontal bottom, straight vertical right edge). The left side has exactly TWO sharp points sticking out, and BOTH aim perfectly straight HORIZONTALLY to the left (one at the top, one in the middle).

Diamond head. The entire left outline is one single, continuous, straight DIAGONAL line slanting down-left to the lowest point. It is absolutely NOT vertical. Just below the head, a perfectly flat horizontal top edge extends straight out to the RIGHT. There are NO vertical edges and NO flat horizontal bottom edges anywhere.

Diamond head at the top right. The left side features a prominent sharp triangle pointing perfectly horizontally left. A sharp point also sticks out to the right side. The absolute lowest part is a sharp tip pointing diagonally down-left (not straight down). NO flat bottom edges and NO straight vertical edges.

Highest point is the top tip of a central diamond. Attached to its left is a sharp triangle aiming perfectly straight HORIZONTALLY left. Extending from below the diamond is ONLY ONE thick block, slanting diagonally DOWN-RIGHT. The sharp tip at the far bottom-right is the ONLY lowest point of the entire figure. There is NO leg pointing down-left (the space below the horizontally left-pointing triangle is empty). NO flat horizontal bottom edges anywhere.

NO diamond. Perfect 90-degree bottom-RIGHT corner (flat horizontal bottom, straight vertical right edge). The left side has exactly TWO sharp points sticking out, and BOTH aim perfectly straight HORIZONTALLY to the left (one at the top, one in the middle).

Absolutely NO diamond shape anywhere. Looks like a solid paper airplane pointing straight horizontally left. The highest point is a single sharp peak. Its ENTIRE back-right outline is one continuous, perfectly straight DIAGONAL line slanting smoothly down and to the right, from the top peak to the lowest bottom-right tip. NO flat horizontal edges and NO vertical straight edges anywhere.

Diamond head. The bottom-RIGHT corner is a perfect 90-degree angle formed by a perfectly flat horizontal bottom edge and a straight vertical right edge. The leftmost point of the entire figure aims diagonally down-left (it does NOT point straight horizontally left).

NO diamond. Perfect 90-degree bottom-RIGHT corner (flat horizontal bottom, straight vertical right edge). The left side has exactly TWO sharp points sticking out, and BOTH aim perfectly straight HORIZONTALLY to the left (one at the top, one in the middle).

Diamond head. The entire left outline is one single, continuous, straight DIAGONAL line slanting down-left to the lowest point. It is absolutely NOT vertical. Just below the head, a perfectly flat horizontal top edge extends straight out to the RIGHT. There are NO vertical edges and NO flat horizontal bottom edges anywhere.

Diamond head. Left side has a sharp triangle pointing perfectly horizontally left. Right side has a sharp point sticking out. Crucially, it has EXACTLY ONE LEG at the bottom, which points diagonally down-left and is the absolute lowest point. It DOES NOT have a second leg pointing down-right.

Diamond head. Left side has a sharp triangle pointing perfectly horizontally left. Crucially, it has EXACTLY ONE LEG extending from the bottom, which points diagonally DOWN-RIGHT and is the absolute lowest point. It DOES NOT have a second leg pointing down-left.

Diamond head. The bottom-right corner is a perfect 90-degree angle, formed by a flat horizontal bottom edge and a straight vertical right edge. The leftmost part points diagonally down-left (not horizontally left). Make sure the straight vertical edge is on the right!

Target 7: NO diamond shape. Solid arrowhead/paper airplane pointing perfectly horizontally LEFT. Its ENTIRE back-RIGHT outline is one continuous, perfectly straight DIAGONAL line slanting from the top peak all the way down to the bottom-right tip. It has ABSOLUTELY NO flat horizontal edges and NO vertical straight edges anywhere. (Make sure it points LEFT; do NOT pick the shape pointing right with a flat bottom).
